title: CC and BCC
drDigitalIntro: CC (Carbon Copy) sends a copy of an email to someone in addition to the main recipient. Everyone can see who was CC'd. BCC (Blind Carbon Copy) sends a copy to someone without anyone else knowing they got it — the recipient isn't shown to others. Use CC to keep people informed and BCC when you want someone to see an email privately.

playgroundTask.question: You send an email and add Bob to the CC field. What does this mean?

options.text: Bob gets a copy and everyone can see he received it
Bob gets a secret copy that no one knows about

drDigitalSuccess: CC = visible copy. Everyone in the email thread can see that Bob was included. BCC = invisible copy — only Bob knows.
drDigitalHint: CC = Carbon Copy (visible). BCC = Blind Carbon Copy (secret). Blind means nobody can see it.
